title: Calculate your expenses – Home office expenses for employees - Canada.ca
content:
## Home office expenses for employees
- Go to navigation menu
- Skip to main content

## Calculate your expenses
To understand the math behind the home office expenses calculation, refer to how the claim is calculated .
To use the calculator, select from the options below.
The CRA is not collecting or retaining any of the information you enter on this page. These amounts are only
				used to help you determine the amount of work space expenses you may be eligible to claim.
Warning: This form does not collect or store your information.
Answer a few questions to begin calculating your home office expenses.
Before you continue, make sure you have:
- confirmed you meet the eligibility criteria for the temporary flat rate method to make a claim
- understood this claim method What is the temporary flat rate
$0.00 is the total amount you can claim for the temporary flat rate.
$2.00 multiplied by 0 days worked at home
This amount will go on line 9939 of Form T777S , which will also go on line 22900 of your return.
Before you continue, make sure you have:
- confirmed you meet the eligibility criteria for the detailed method to make a claim
- total size of your work space
- total size of all finished areas in your home
							(including your work space)
- supporting documents for all of the expenses you
							can claim for the period(s) you worked from home
- documents that show your employment income and
							deductions (e.g. T4 slip)
- Form T2200S signed and completed by your
							employer
Note: Given the various types of employment expenses, the calculator below can only
							help you determine the employment-use portion of your work-space-in-the-home-expenses. The calculator
							will provide you with the values for lines 42 to 46 and 48 of Form T777.
You will still need to complete the remainder of Form T777 to determine the final amount you can claim
							on your tax return.
Before you continue, make sure you have:
- confirmed you meet the eligibility criteria for the detailed method to make a claim
- total size of your work space
- total size of all finished areas in your home
							(including your work space)
- supporting documents for all of the expenses you
							can claim for the period(s) you worked from home
- Form T2200 signed and completed by your employer
What is your residence situation?
- Rent, and have not moved while working from home within the year
- Own, and have not moved while working from home within the year
- Changed your residence or work spaces while working from home within the year
Example: Moving between two homes
If this situation applies, you will need to calculate the expenses for each work space location
						separately to get the correct amount you can claim. This means you will use the calculator multiple times.
You will only need to complete one Form T777 S . Add the amounts
						you calculated for each work space location together when filling in the lines on the form.
Your work space is in a home that you
Did you earn commission income (Box 42 on T4 slip)?
What are the limitations for claiming expenses when earning commissions
What type of space do you work from at home?
Difference between a designated work space (room) and a common
					(shared) space
- Designated work space (room)
- Common (shared) space
Choose your preferred measurement units

## Your home office expenses results
Print and keep this record using the print preview button below . You will need the
						amounts below when filling out the T777 S form.

### Summary of total expenses
$0.00 is the total expenses you
							can claim.
This amount will go on line 5 on the T777S form, which
							will also go on line
							22900 of your return.

### Summary of the employment-use amount
$0.00 is the employment-use
						portion.
This amount will go on line 12 line 48 of the T777 S form.
The total amount of the employment-use portion is based on:
- $0.00 expenses paid for the
							entire home and work space $0.00 × 0 %
- $0.00 expenses paid for the work
							space only $0.00 × 0 %
T777 S form for 2023
Supplies (postage, stationery, ink cartridge, other office supplies, etc.)
Other expenses (employment use of a cell phone, long distance calls for employment purposes,
											etc.)
Total expenses (you can claim)
Electricity, heat, water, home Internet access fees
Maintenance (cleaning supplies, light bulbs, etc.) Maintenance of entire home (including work space) = [$0.00] Designated work space (room) only = [$0.00] Common (shared) work space only = [$0.00]
- Maintenance of entire home (including work space) = [$0.00]
- Designated work space (room) only = [$0.00]
- Common (shared) work space only = [$0.00]
Home insurance (commission income only)
Property taxes (commission income only)
Other expenses (rent, etc.)
Employment-use amount
Total of Supplies, Other expenses, annual dues (line 21200 of return) and RPP deductions (line
											20700 of return)
Expenses available to use in future years
Size of work space in your home
Hours a week in work space
Total finished area of your home
The income you reported is less than the expenses claimed for employment-use. You can carry forward [$0.00] to be claimed in future years.
This amount will go on line 16 of the T777S form.
Note: If you did not earn enough commission income
								to cover your expenses, you may claim expenses as a salaried employee instead.
You are now ready to print these amounts so you can enter them into the form. Keep your
					printed results in case we review your claim later and ask to see your calculations.
The data is not saved on this page. If you leave this page without printing you will need to enter your
					information again.
Print preview work space
					in home expenses statement
- If you are a commission employee , you may not be able to claim your total work-space-in-the-home expenses if they exceed your commission income. For more information, refer to: T4404 – Chapter 2 – Employees earning commission income

### Example of working from the dining room table
- You can only claim the expenses you paid in the part of the year you worked from home. You cannot claim the expenses you paid for the whole year. Example: Multiple periods working from home Working situation Kim worked from home from March 15 to May 31, 2025 . During these months, she worked exclusively from home and performed all of the duties that she normally performed at her employer’s office. Kim went back to the office full-time in June. Kim worked exclusively from home again in November and December. successful Result Because Kim mainly (more than 50% of the time) worked from home during one or more periods of at least a month (four consecutive weeks) in 2025 (in this case, the first period was 11 weeks and the second period was 9 weeks ), she may be able to claim the work-space-in-the-home expenses she paid for those periods if she meets the other conditions. Period 1: 2025-03-15 to 2025-05-31 Period 2: 2025-11-01 to 2025-12-31
You can only claim the expenses you paid in the part of the year you worked from home. You cannot claim the expenses you paid for the whole year.
Kim worked from home from March 15 to May 31, 2025 . During these months, she worked exclusively from home and performed all of the duties that she normally performed at her employer’s office.
Kim went back to the office full-time in June.
Kim worked exclusively from home again in November and December.
Because Kim mainly (more than 50% of the time) worked from home during one or more periods of at least a month (four consecutive weeks) in 2025 (in this case, the first period was 11 weeks and the second period was 9 weeks ), she may be able to claim the work-space-in-the-home expenses she paid for those periods if she meets the other conditions.
- Period 1: 2025-03-15 to 2025-05-31
- Period 2: 2025-11-01 to 2025-12-31
- Expenses you paid that relate to the work space as well as other areas of the home . You can claim the percentage of those expenses that relate to the work space. Example: Minor repairs of the home furnace or air-conditioner or the purchase of household cleaning products.
- Example: Minor repairs of the home furnace or air-conditioner or the purchase of household cleaning products.
- Expenses related to a part of the house that you did not use as a work space . You cannot claim any part of those expenses. Example: Repainting a bedroom that is not used for work.
- Example: Repainting a bedroom that is not used for work.
- Expenses related to the work space only . You can claim the total amount of the expenses if the amount paid is reasonable. Example: Purchase of light bulbs, repainting the work space, or repairing walls or ceilings after the installation of phones, fax machines or other office equipment you used in the work space.
- Example: Purchase of light bulbs, repainting the work space, or repairing walls or ceilings after the installation of phones, fax machines or other office equipment you used in the work space.
- Expenses related to a part of the house that you did not use as a work space . You cannot claim any part of those expenses. Example: Repainting a bedroom that is not used for work.
- Example: Repainting a bedroom that is not used for work.
Basic service plan ( minutes/data )
Yes if all conditions are met | Salaried employees Yes if all conditions are met | Commissioned employees Conditions: The cost of the plan is reasonable The cost of the plan has been reasonably apportioned between employment and personal use You are able to show the cellular minutes or data you consumed directly while performing your employment duties (as well as the cost of the minutes or data )
- Yes if all conditions are met | Salaried employees
- Yes if all conditions are met | Commissioned employees
